name: Featuretypevalues
description:
Features in banking and business can be classified into different categories based on their purpose, functionality, and the value they provide. Here's a structured way to classify them:

1. Based on Functionality
Core Features: Essential functionalities required for the basic operation of a product or service.
Examples: Account creation, deposits, withdrawals, balance inquiries in banking.
Value-Added Features: Additional services that enhance customer experience and satisfaction.
Examples: Cashback rewards, financial planning tools, or loyalty programs.

2. Based on Service Type
Customer-Facing Features: Visible to customers and directly used by them.
Examples: Mobile banking apps, self-service kiosks, and chatbots.
Back-End Features: Operational features that support business processes but are not directly visible to customers.
Examples: Fraud detection systems, transaction monitoring, and risk assessment tools.


3. Based on Delivery Channel
Digital Features: Offered through online platforms or apps.
Examples: Biometric login, real-time notifications, and online fund transfers.
Physical Features: Provided at physical locations or through tangible means.
Examples: ATM cash withdrawal, in-branch consultations.

4. Based on the Target Audience
Retail Features: Designed for individual customers or small businesses.
Examples: Personal loans, savings accounts, and credit cards.
Corporate Features: Tailored for businesses or large organizations.
Examples: Cash management solutions, trade financing, and payroll processing.

5. Based on Purpose
Transactional Features: Facilitate day-to-day transactions.
Examples: Payments, transfers, or POS (Point of Sale) services.
Analytical Features: Provide insights, reports, or tracking capabilities.
Examples: Expense categorization, investment performance analysis.
Support Features: Assist customers in resolving issues or queries.
Examples: 24/7 customer support, AI-powered chatbots.

6. Based on Innovation Level
Standard Features: Common across most competitors in the industry.
Examples: Basic savings accounts, ATM access.
Differentiating Features: Unique or advanced features that set a company apart.
Examples: Cryptocurrency wallets, eco-friendly investment options.

7. Based on Technology
AI-Driven Features: Leverage artificial intelligence for smarter operations.
Examples: Fraud detection, personalized financial advice.
Cloud-Based Features: Hosted on cloud platforms for better scalability and availability.
Examples: Online document storage, remote account management.

8. Based on Compliance
Regulatory Features: Designed to comply with laws and regulations.
Examples: KYC (Know Your Customer) verification, AML (Anti-Money Laundering) checks.
Security Features: Protect customer data and transactions.
Examples: Encryption, two-factor authentication.

name: Agreementtypevalues
description:
A list of values which specifies the category of agreement. Agreement type is very often referred to product. But, the term product does not cover all kinds of agreements. An Agreement can be subtyped from the perspective of various aspects of interest. One can classify the agreement from the perspective of format type (e.g., verbal agreement, written agreement). Other one can classify agreement based on duration like short-term agreement or long-term agreement.

An Agreement can be subtyped from the perspective of various aspects of interest. One can classify the agreement from the perspective of format type (e.g., verbal agreement, written agreement). Another one can classify the same agreement from the perspective of the main subject (e.g., resource agreement, party agreement, product agreement) of agreement and still another one may be interested in a classification according to the type of transaction (e.g., purchase agreement, sale agreement, maintenance agreement), or a combination of these (e.g. resource maintenance agreement). These subtypes are not mutual exclusive. This means that one and the same Agreement can be classified into one or more classifications.
